brief_justification: Wrong answer (No instead of Yes) but the causal reasoning framework applied was sophisticated and internally consistent; Multi-level analysis tracing do-calculus, DAG structure, and distinguishing intervention from observation; Some self-monitoring via the do vs observe distinction and suppression signals; Verified by contrasting interventional vs observational distributions; Acknowledged the distinction matters but was overly confident in the wrong interpretation; Explicitly compared do(W) vs observe(W) scenarios with reasons for rejecting the observational interpretation; Full derivation chain from DAG to do-calculus to conclusion is reproducible, though the premise that do() is the right formalization may be debatable given the question's phrasing
